summary: Gruber reviews the 2020 MacBook Air, arguing it fixes all three major shortcomings of the 2018 retina model: the butterfly keyboard, the $1,199 starting price, and underwhelming performance. The new scissor-switch keyboard feels excellent, the base price returns to $999 with double the storage, and the quad-core CPU option delivers meaningful performance gains. He notes minor quibbles — no right-side USB-C port, a bad webcam shared across all MacBooks — but concludes the machine is nearly perfect for its intended audience. The review frames this as Apple finally delivering the MacBook Air the lineup always needed.
key_points: The 2020 MacBook Air fixes all three shortcomings of the 2018 model: butterfly keyboard, high price, and weak performance
The base model returns to $999 with 256 GB storage (previously $1,300 for that configuration), making it easy to recommend again
The scissor-switch keyboard feels even slightly better than the 16-inch MacBook Pro's, conclusively proving sub-1mm key travel was a mistake
The quad-core i5 option delivers strong single-core performance competitive with the A12X iPad Pro, though Intel still trails ARM in performance-per-watt
The only real complaints — webcam quality, no right-side USB-C — apply to all MacBooks or are trivial
money_quotes: With the new 2020 MacBook Air, Apple has pulled a Michael Corleone and settled all family business.
The MacBook Air is supposed to start at $999. It just is.
What's important, I think, is that it's a good/faster/fastest lineup — not meh/good/faster.
To my taste, this conclusively proves that less than 1 mm travel is too little travel.
Apple is once again making excellent, world-class, no-caveat MacBook keyboards, so something, however insignificant in the grand scheme of life, is right in the world.
The keyboard has gone from 'well, it's OK' to 'damn, this keyboard feels so good it makes me want to write something'.
I've never met anyone who wishes that the MacBook Air had the Touch Bar.
Don't overthink it. The new MacBook Air is what it looks like: nearly perfect.
key_insight: The 2020 MacBook Air finally delivers what the MacBook Air was always supposed to be — a great computer at the right price with no caveats — by fixing the keyboard, dropping the price, and meaningfully improving performance.
